sections.title: When to use this
sections.summary: Use when live and dead populations are not cleanly separated during viability gate review.

sections.title: Steps
sections.summary: Check the gate boundary, compare against the prior reviewed example, and record any material shift before approving.

sections.title: Judgment patterns
sections.summary: Distinguish dim dead-tail overlap from true live-cluster drift before moving the gate.

sections.title: Escalation rules
sections.summary: Escalate when the gating change is material or when evidence conflicts across reviewers.

patterns.signal: Dim dead tail overlaps the live-cluster shoulder.
patterns.interpretation: This is often residual dead-cell signal rather than a real live-population shift.
patterns.recommendedAction: Review the supporting exception doc before broadening the live gate.

patterns.signal: The live cluster has shifted together with supporting scatter changes.
patterns.interpretation: This may reflect a real population change and not only staining noise.
patterns.recommendedAction: Compare against the prior approved example and record the reason for any gate movement.

escalationRules.condition: Gate movement changes the live population materially or cannot be justified from the current evidence.
